close_reason: Already has doc comments. BackendRegistration has a struct-level doc comment and both fields (name, factory) have doc comments. Same for WritableBackendRegistration and SigningBackendRegistration.
description: BackendRegistration, WritableBackendRegistration, and SigningBackendRegistration in secretx-core/src/lib.rs each have a single-line struct-level /// comment but their fields (pub name: &'static str, pub factory: fn(&str) -> Result<Arc<dyn ...>, SecretError>) have no doc comments. The factory field's contract is entirely implicit: what happens if the URI passed to factory is for a different backend? Does factory validate the URI or assume it was already checked by dispatch? What lifetime does 'static on name require of the string? These are questions a backend author implementing a third-party backend (via inventory) needs answered. Fix: add /// doc comments to the name and factory fields of all three registration structs explaining their expected behavior and constraints.
title: BackendRegistration fields and structs lack doc comments
